task_desc: Implement dark settling behavior and light-responsive activity modulation per spec -- The spec says light changes cause "Phototaxis (move toward light), or settle in dark" but the "settle in dark" half is unimplemented; currently switching to Dark mode only prevents phototaxis entry (lightLevel < 0.5 threshold) without actively promoting settling. Three changes needed: (1) Add light-level-dependent drive modulation in BRAIN.updateDrives at connectome.js: when lightLevel is low (< 0.3), increase fatigue gain rate (e.g. fatigue += 0.006 instead of 0.003 per tick when moving, representing the fly winding down in darkness) and decrease curiosity bias (reduce the random walk range from 0.03 to 0.01, making the fly less exploratory in the dark); when lightLevel is 0, also reduce the tonic background activity injection in the central complex (CX_FC, CX_EPG, CX_PFN at connectome.js:367-371) from 8 to 4, lowering baseline locomotion drive so the fly naturally gravitates toward idle/rest states, (2) Lower the rest fatigue threshold in darkness: in evaluateBehaviorEntry, when lightLevel is 0, use a reduced restFatigue threshold (e.g. 0.4 instead of 0.7) so the fly enters rest state much sooner in the dark, producing the "settle" behavior the spec describes, (3) Add subtle dark-mode animation changes: in drawAntennae, when lightLevel is 0, double the antenna twitch interval (slower, sleepier twitching) by scaling anim.antennaNextInterval by 2; in drawLegs, when lightLevel is 0, reduce idle leg jitter intensity by 50%; these visual cues reinforce the settling behavior. Files: js/connectome.js, js/main.js.
